channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Skull and Bones Reveals Year 3 Roadmap and New Features, With a Developer AMA Tonight
item.content:encoded:
We’re finally getting a look at what Skull and Bones Year 3 looks like, with a new roadmap, Year 3 stream, and an AMA set for tonight.

Ubisoft Singapore team held a stream to lay out Year 3 season 1, as well as some of what else they're developing for later in the year. The team has been teasing some of these additions recently, with only a little context, and now we get to learn more. 

First up is a new large ship, the Galleon. This is a huge ship that they’re calling a “broadside brawler”.  Sometime later, we will get two more new large ships: the Junk, which they refer to in the trailer above as “Inferno Juggernaut”, and the Fluyt, “Guardian of the Fleet”.

Year 3, Season 1 goes live May 12th and offers a new power progression system called the Season MasteryTree and associated perks to go along with it. Each season, new loadouts become available, by way of these new perks and build options. The trailer has a few examples that will be possible with the new feature.

For those who want some new challenges, World Tiers are coming to offer guidance and a path for beginners and those who want to get better and face tougher stuff 

Shallow Waters will be for ship rank 4-12. Cutthroat Seas, for rank 12-17, if for those ready to step into Faction War and competition. Rogue Storms, for 17-22, is for those looking to master their ship and take on additional new season challenges and face enemies that have powerful modifiers. Finally, Brutal Tempest is for rank 22-30, with endgame content at a difficulty for those who want to prove themselves against the hardest content Skull and Bones has to offer. 

Speaking of new content, Trials are coming sometime in the season, new Abyssal Depths and elite boss challenges, quality of life improvements, and more. Also new power progression systems that offer mod transfer and equipment sets. 

You can watch the full Y3 stream here.

Today, the Skull and Bones dev team is holding an AMA on Reddit. You can still submit questions here, and come back when the team arrives at 8:00 p.m. Pacific Time / 11:00 p.m. Eastern. 

They plan to talk about all things year three, expanding upon the seasonal Mastery system they just unveiled, world tiers, ships, naval combat, and more.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/skull-and-bones-reveals-year-3-roadmap-and-new-features-with-a-developer-ama-tonight-2000138000
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Shadow Drops Star Fox Direct; Announces New Star Fox Game For Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo shadow dropped a whole Star Fox direct today, announcing a remake of the iconic Star Fox 64, simply named 'Star Fox, set to release this June.

The upcoming game set in the Lylat System is a "cinematic take" on the iconic Star Fox 64, often considered one of the best Nintendo games of all time (especially by me). The game, which brings all new visuals, features, cutscenes and dialogue, recreates the battle against Andross as Fox McCloud and company aim to defend the Lylat system. 

As a result, characters are overhauled and look quite a bit more "animal-like" than we might remember, and some of the dialogue that has stood the test of time since its 1997 release has been retooled, as showcased in the gameplay video in the Direct. Each mission layout is the same as the N64 release, preserving the gameplay that many of us grew up on. 

Star Fox also brings a 4-vs-4 Battle Mode that can be played locally or online with friends, and it also incorporates Nintendo's Game Share program, meaning one copy can be shared with up to three friends on Nintendo Switch 2 and the original Nintendo Switch, so you and your friends can defend the Lylat system together. 

Interestingly, we're also seeing some of the Nvidia Tegra chip's AR capabilities leveraged here with Game Chat, as you can take on the guise of Star Fox or Star Wolf's team of fighter pilots, so long as you have a camera attached to your Switch 2, making for some very... interesting-looking game chat windows. 

Star Fox will also incorporate the Nintendo Switch 2's Joy Con 2 Mouse controls, and it will be compatible with the Nintendo Switch Online N64 controller, so those of us who want the nostalgia can play the game as we originally did back in the day.

Star Fox will release on Nintendo Switch 2 on June 25th. Catch the gameplay reveal below. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/nintendo-shadow-drops-star-fox-direct-announces-new-star-fox-game-for-switch-2-2000138001
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
